name: Fête de la Musique
description: France's national music celebration — free live performances across every street and genre.
historicalNote: Launched in 1982 by Jack Lang, Minister of Culture, as a way to bring music to every corner of France. What started as a Parisian idea has spread to over 120 countries.

name: Cannes Film Festival
description: The world's most prestigious film festival with red carpets, premieres, and the legendary Palme d'Or.
historicalNote: Founded in 1946 as an alternative to the Venice Film Festival. The Palme d'Or has been awarded to legendary directors like Quentin Tarantino, Francis Ford Coppola, and Bong Joon-ho.
frenchPhrase: Le cinéma, c'est l'écriture moderne dont l'encre est la lumière
phraseTranslation: Cinema is modern writing whose ink is light — Jean Cocteau

name: Nice Carnival
description: Spectacular flower battles and illuminated parades with massive artistic floats on the Côte d'Azur.
historicalNote: Dating back to 1294, the Nice Carnival is one of the oldest in the world. The tradition of flower battles (Bataille de Fleurs) began in 1876.

name: Bastille Day Celebrations
description: Military parade on the Champs-Élysées and breathtaking fireworks at the Eiffel Tower.
historicalNote: Celebrates the storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789 — the pivotal moment of the French Revolution and the birth of modern democracy in France.

name: Fête des Lumières
description: Lyon transforms into an open-air gallery with stunning light installations and projections.
historicalNote: Originated in 1852 when citizens spontaneously lit candles in their windows to celebrate the Virgin Mary. Today it attracts 4 million visitors over 4 nights.
frenchPhrase: La lumière est le premier des peintres
phraseTranslation: Light is the first of all painters

name: Avignon Theatre Festival
description: Over 1,000 shows in medieval Avignon — from contemporary theatre to performances in the Palace of the Popes.
historicalNote: Founded in 1947 by Jean Vilar in the courtyard of the Palace of the Popes. It revolutionized French theatre by making it accessible to all social classes.
frenchPhrase: Tout le monde est un théâtre
phraseTranslation: All the world is a stage

name: Bordeaux Wine Festival
description: Taste hundreds of wines from legendary Bordeaux châteaux along the Garonne river.
historicalNote: Bordeaux has been a wine capital since Roman times. The region's 7,000 châteaux produce over 700 million bottles annually, making it the world's finest wine region.
frenchPhrase: Dans le vin, il y a la vérité

name: Strasbourg Christmas Market
description: France's oldest Christmas market (1570) with twinkling stalls, mulled wine, and Alsatian specialties.
historicalNote: Founded in 1570, the Christkindelsmärik is the oldest Christmas market in France and one of the oldest in Europe. Strasbourg proudly holds the title 'Capital of Christmas.'
frenchPhrase: Noël n'est pas un jour ni une saison. C'est un état d'esprit
phraseTranslation: Christmas is not a day or a season. It's a state of mind

name: Paris Fashion Week
description: Top designers showcase their latest haute couture creations in the fashion capital of the world.
historicalNote: Paris has been the fashion capital since the 17th century when Louis XIV's court set trends for all of Europe. Paris Fashion Week began formally in 1973.
frenchPhrase: La mode se démode, le style jamais
phraseTranslation: Fashion fades, style is eternal — Yves Saint Laurent

name: Lemon Festival of Menton
description: Massive sculptures made of lemons and oranges fill the gardens with citrus float parades.
historicalNote: Started in 1934 when Menton was the largest lemon producer in Europe. Each year uses over 140 tonnes of citrus fruit to create the stunning displays.
frenchPhrase: Quand la vie te donne des citrons, fais de la limonade
phraseTranslation: When life gives you lemons, make lemonade

name: Jazz in Marciac
description: A tiny Gers village becomes Europe's jazz capital — world-class musicians under the stars.
historicalNote: Founded in 1978 in a village of just 1,300 people. Has hosted legends like Dizzy Gillespie, Wynton Marsalis, and Diana Krall. Proof that great culture needs no big city.

name: Mont-Blanc Ultra Trail
description: 170km ultra-trail around Mont-Blanc through France, Italy, and Switzerland.
historicalNote: The UTMB was created in 2003 and has grown into the world's most prestigious ultra-trail race, attracting 10,000 runners from 100+ countries.
frenchPhrase: La montagne enseigne ce que les livres ne peuvent pas
phraseTranslation: The mountain teaches what books cannot

name: Tour de France — Paris Finish
description: The grand finale — the peloton sprints down the Champs-Élysées to crown the yellow jersey winner.
historicalNote: The Tour de France began in 1903 to boost newspaper sales. It has become the most watched annual sporting event in the world with 3.5 billion TV viewers.
frenchPhrase: Le vélo est la plus noble invention de l'humanité
phraseTranslation: The bicycle is humanity's noblest invention

name: Épernay Champagne Harvest Festival
description: Champagne cellar tours, tastings from legendary houses on the Avenue de Champagne.
historicalNote: Épernay's Avenue de Champagne is called 'the most drinkable street in the world.' Beneath it lie 110km of champagne cellars holding millions of bottles.
frenchPhrase: Je ne bois que du champagne quand je suis heureuse et quand je suis triste
phraseTranslation: I drink champagne when I'm happy and when I'm sad — Madame Bollinger

name: Marseille Street Art Festival
description: International street artists transform Marseille's walls and alleyways into urban masterpieces.
historicalNote: Marseille, France's oldest city (600 BC), has reinvented itself as a cultural hotspot. After being European Capital of Culture in 2013, its street art scene has exploded.
frenchPhrase: L'art est partout, il suffit de regarder
phraseTranslation: Art is everywhere, you just have to look

name: Nuit Blanche
description: Paris stays awake all night — free art installations and performances from sunset to sunrise.
historicalNote: Created in 2002 by the City of Paris. Nuit Blanche (White Night) has inspired similar events in over 30 cities worldwide including Toronto, Brussels, and Melbourne.
frenchPhrase: Paris est toujours une bonne idée
phraseTranslation: Paris is always a good idea — Audrey Hepburn

name: Les Chorégies d'Orange
description: Opera and classical concerts in the ancient Roman Theatre of Orange with legendary acoustics.
historicalNote: The Chorégies d'Orange is the oldest active festival in France, dating back to 1869. The Roman theatre was built in the 1st century AD.
frenchPhrase: La musique est la langue des émotions
phraseTranslation: Music is the language of emotions — Emmanuel Kant

name: Lavender Festival of Valensole
description: Endless purple lavender fields with local markets, distillery visits, and Provençal charm.
historicalNote: Provençal lavender cultivation dates to the 1920s. The Valensole plateau is the largest lavender-growing area in France, producing essential oils prized worldwide.
frenchPhrase: La Provence est un poème que le soleil a écrit
phraseTranslation: Provence is a poem written by the sun

name: Galette des Rois Season
description: France celebrates Epiphany with the Galette des Rois — find the hidden figurine and become king!
historicalNote: This tradition dates to the 14th century. The Élysée Palace receives a giant galette each year — but one without a figurine, because 'the President should not be crowned.'
frenchPhrase: Qui mange la fève est roi
phraseTranslation: Whoever finds the charm is king

name: Fête de la Gastronomie
description: Lyon celebrates its culinary heritage with chef demos, market tours, and truffle tastings.
historicalNote: Lyon's bouchon tradition dates to the 16th century. The city has more restaurants per capita than any French city and was home to Paul Bocuse, the 'Chef of the Century.'
frenchPhrase: Dis-moi ce que tu manges, je te dirai qui tu es
phraseTranslation: Tell me what you eat, and I'll tell you who you are — Brillat-Savarin

name: Mont Saint-Michel High Tides
description: Witness the abbey become an island again during the equinox supertides — nature's grandest spectacle in Europe.
historicalNote: The abbey was founded in 708 AD. For centuries, pilgrims waited for low tide to cross the treacherous sands, giving it the nickname 'St. Michael in peril of the sea.'
phraseTranslation: The tide waits for no one

name: Inter-Celtic Festival of Lorient
description: The world's largest gathering of Celtic nations with 4,500 artists, bagpipes, and massive parades.
historicalNote: Founded in 1971, it celebrates the cultural links between Brittany, Scotland, Ireland, Wales, Cornwall, and Galicia. Attracts over 800,000 visitors annually.

name: Fêtes de Bayonne
description: France's biggest party — 1 million people dressed in white with red scarves celebrating Basque culture.
historicalNote: The tradition began in 1932, inspired by Pamplona's San Fermín. The city keys are thrown from the town hall balcony to the crowd to open the 5-day celebration.

name: Carcassonne Festival
description: Concerts and theatre inside the UNESCO-listed medieval fortress walls under the stars.
historicalNote: Carcassonne is Europe's largest fortified city. The festival takes place in the Jean-Deschamps Theatre, nestled within the ancient double walls built in the 13th century.
frenchPhrase: Il ne faut pas mourir sans avoir vu Carcassonne
phraseTranslation: One must not die without seeing Carcassonne — Nadaud

name: 24 Hours of Le Mans
description: The world's oldest and most famous endurance car race — a test of speed and stamina.
historicalNote: First held in 1923, it is the ultimate test of man and machine. The 'Mulsanne Straight' allowed cars to reach 400 km/h before chicanes were added in 1990.

name: Route du Rhum Departure
description: Watch the majestic departure of giant trimarans for the legendary solo transatlantic race to Guadeloupe.
historicalNote: Held every 4 years since 1978. It is France's most popular sailing event, with over 2 million spectators gathering on the coast to watch the start.
frenchPhrase: Homme libre, toujours tu chériras la mer
phraseTranslation: Free man, you will always cherish the sea — Baudelaire

name: Annecy Animation Festival
description: The 'Cannes of Animation' — open-air screenings by the lake and world premieres.
historicalNote: Founded in 1960. Disney, Pixar, and Ghibli often premiere films here. The nightly outdoor screenings on the Pâquier lawn beside the lake are legendary.
frenchPhrase: L'imagination est plus importante que le savoir
phraseTranslation: Imagination is more important than knowledge

name: Great Roman Games
description: Europe's biggest historical reenactment in the world's best-preserved Roman amphitheatre.
historicalNote: Nîmes was a major Roman colony. The Games feature 500 reenactors from all over Europe recreating gladiator fights and chariot races with historical accuracy.
phraseTranslation: I came, I saw, I conquered — Julius Caesar

name: Provins Medieval Festival
description: Step back into the Middle Ages with knights, troubadours, and artisans in a UNESCO walled city.
historicalNote: Provins was a major trade hub in the 12th century, famous for its fairs. The festival is the largest medieval fair in France, attracting 100,000 costumed visitors.
phraseTranslation: Hear ye, hear ye, good people!

name: Deauville American Film Festival
description: Hollywood comes to Normandy — stars, plush beach huts, and American cinema premieres.
historicalNote: Founded in 1975. Deauville's beach boardwalk is lined with cabins bearing the names of Hollywood legends who have visited, like Clint Eastwood and Morgan Freeman.
frenchPhrase: Le cinéma est un rêve éveillé

name: Fête de la Musique
description: Celebrated on the summer solstice, free concerts take over every street corner, bar, and park in France.
historicalNote: Initiated by Jack Lang in 1982 to democratize music. It is now celebrated in over 120 countries worldwide.
phraseTranslation: Make music! (A play on words with Fête de la Musique)

name: Tour de France Final Stage
description: The world's most famous cycling race concludes with a sprint finish on the Champs-Élysées.
historicalNote: The first Tour was in 1903. The finish on the Champs-Élysées has been a tradition since 1975.
phraseTranslation: The Big Loop (Nickname for the Tour de France)

name: Festival d'Avignon
description: One of the world's greatest theater festivals, held in the courtyard of the Popes' Palace and various locations.
historicalNote: Founded in 1947 by Jean Vilar. The 'In' festival is official, while the 'Off' festival fills the streets with thousands of independent shows.
frenchPhrase: Le monde entier est un théâtre

name: Nice Carnival
description: Giant floats, flower battles, and illuminated parades on the French Riviera.
historicalNote: One of the oldest carnivals in the world, mentioned as early as 1294. The 'Battle of Flowers' highlights the region's floral production.

name: Menton Lemon Festival
description: Unique festival featuring sculptures made entirely of lemons and oranges.
historicalNote: Started in 1934 to promote Menton's citrus industry. Over 140 tons of citrus fruit are used for the displays.

name: Fête des Lumières
description: Lyon lights up for four nights with spectacular light installations on monuments and buildings.
historicalNote: Originates from a 1643 promise to the Virgin Mary to save the city from plague. Locals place candles in their windows on December 8th.

name: Strasbourg Christmas Market
description: The 'Capital of Christmas' hosts France's oldest and most famous Christmas market.
historicalNote: Established in 1570 as the 'Christkindelsmärik'. The Great Christmas Tree in Place Kléber is a central symbol.

name: Bordeaux Wine Festival
description: A 2km wine trail along the Garonne river celebrating Bordeaux and Aquitaine wines.
historicalNote: Held biennially (even years). Visitors buy a tasting pass and sample wines from over 80 appellations.

name: Dunkirk Carnival
description: A raucous, community-driven carnival where locals dress in drag and march behind drum majors, celebrating fishermen.
historicalNote: Dates back to the 17th century as a feast for fishermen leaving for Iceland. Famous for the 'herring throw' from the city hall balcony.

name: Chorégies d'Orange
description: The oldest festival in France, featuring opera and classical music in a Roman theater.
historicalNote: Created in 1869. The Roman Theatre's acoustic wall provides exceptional sound quality without amplification.

name: Versailles Fountains Night Show
description: The gardens of the Château come alive with illuminated fountains and baroque music fireworks.
historicalNote: Recreates the grand fêtes of Louis XIV. The hydraulic system is still largely the original one from the 17th century.

name: Puy du Fou Cinéscénie
description: The world's biggest night show with substantial fireworks, thousands of actors, and special effects.
historicalNote: Recounts the history of the Vendée region from the Middle Ages to WWII through the eyes of a peasant family.

name: Lavender Fields Bloom
description: Witness the endless purple fields of Provence in full bloom.
historicalNote: Lavender has been harvested here for centuries for perfume and soap. The Plateau de Valensole offers the most iconic views.

name: La Braderie de Lille
description: Europe's largest flea market, where visitors eat heaps of mussels and fries.
historicalNote: Dates back to the 12th century. Restaurants pile empty mussel shells outside their doors to compete for the biggest mound.

name: Cannes Film Festival
description: Star-studded premieres, red carpets, and the pursuit of the Palme d'Or.
historicalNote: First held in 1946. It is one of the 'Big Three' film festivals alongside Venice and Berlin.

name: Beaujolais Nouveau Day
description: Celebration of the first wine of the season, released at midnight on the third Thursday of November.
historicalNote: Started as a local phenomenon in Beaujolais bars, it became a global marketing success in the 1980s.

name: Printemps de Bourges
description: Major music festival kicking off the season, focusing on contemporary music.
historicalNote: Founded in 1977. It is known for discovering new talent and mixing genres from rock to rap and chanson.

name: Jazz in Marciac
description: World-renowned jazz festival held in a small Gascon bastide town, famous for duck and foie gras.
historicalNote: Established in 1978. It attracts jazz legends like Wynton Marsalis who is the festival's patron.

name: Hellfest
description: One of Europe's biggest heavy metal festivals, known for its unique decor and sculptures.
historicalNote: Founded in 2006 on the ashes of Furyfest. It has become a pilgrimage site for metalheads worldwide.

name: Alsace Wine Harvest
description: Experience the grape harvest along the Wine Route in golden autumn light.
historicalNote: Alsace wines (Riesling, Gewurztraminer) have been produced here since Roman times. The 'Vendanges' is a community effort.
frenchPhrase: Le vin est la poésie de la terre

name: Les Vieilles Charrues
description: France's largest music festival, held in a field in central Brittany.
historicalNote: Started as a small student party in 1992. Now attracts ~280,000 people. 'Vieilles Charrues' means 'Old Ploughs'.

name: Fête de la Coquille Saint-Jacques
description: Celebrate the opening of the scallop fishing season with tastings, markets, and concerts.
historicalNote: The scallop (Coquille St-Jacques) is a symbol of pilgrims walking to Santiago de Compostela.

name: Main Square Festival
description: International pop/rock festival held in the stunning UNESCO-listed Citadel of Arras.
historicalNote: Created in 2004. The Citadel was built by Vauban in the 17th century.

name: Rock en Seine
description: Major rock festival held in the historic Domaine National de Saint-Cloud park.
historicalNote: Founded in 2003. It marks the traditional end of the summer music festival season in France.

name: Chantilly Arts & Elegance
description: A prestigious Concours d'Elegance for classic cars held in the gardens of Château de Chantilly.
historicalNote: Revives the French tradition of Concours d'Elegance from the 1920s.

name: Garorock
description: A massive music festival mixing rock, electro, and hip-hop in the south-west.
historicalNote: Started as a punk/rock festival in 1997. Now attracts 160,000 attendants.

name: Eurockéennes de Belfort
description: Open-air rock festival located on a peninsula between two lakes.
historicalNote: Created in 1989. Known for its strong environmental commitment and scenic location.

name: Francofolies de La Rochelle
description: Festival dedicated entirely to French-language music, set against the backdrop of medieval towers.
historicalNote: Founded in 1985 to promote Francophone song. It has spawned sister festivals in Montreal and Spa.

name: Nuit Blanche
description: An all-night arts festival where museums open for free and installations take over the streets.
historicalNote: Launched in 2002. It inspired similar 'White Night' events in cities around the world.

name: Paris Fashion Week (Fall)
description: The finale of the 'Big Four' fashion weeks, showcasing top couture houses.
historicalNote: Dates back to 1973. It is organized by the Fédération de la Haute Couture et de la Mode.
frenchPhrase: La mode est un éternel recommencement

name: International Garden Festival
description: Landscape architects from around the world create experimental gardens on a yearly theme.
historicalNote: Since 1992, the festival has revitalized garden art. The Château itself overlooks the Loire river.

name: La Roque-d'Anthéron Piano Festival
description: The world's premier piano festival held outdoors under giant plane trees.
historicalNote: Founded in 1981. The acoustic shell in the Parc du Château de Florans is legendary among pianists.

name: Fêtes de la Mirabelle
description: Celebrating the golden Mirabelle plum with parades, markets, and the election of the Mirabelle Queen.
historicalNote: Lorraine produces 70% of the world's Mirabelle plums. The festival began in 1947.

name: Les Nuits de Fourvière
description: Theater, dance, music, and circus arts in the Roman amphitheatres of Lyon.
historicalNote: Revives the ancient tradition of performances on Fourvière hill, dating back to Lugdunum (Roman Lyon).

name: Cannes Yachting Festival
description: Europe's leading in-water boat show, displaying superyachts and luxury vessels.
historicalNote: Founded in 1977. It marks the opening of the nautical season.

name: Fêtes de Gayant
description: Parade of the giant wicker figures 'Gayant' and his family through the streets.
historicalNote: UNESCO designated Masterpiece of Heritage. The tradition dates back to 1530 to celebrate the end of a siege.

name: Paris-Plages
description: The banks of the Seine are transformed into beaches with sand, deckchairs, and palm trees.
historicalNote: Initiated in 2002 for Parisians who couldn't leave the city for summer vacation.

name: Les Fêtes de Saint-Nicolas
description: Grand parade honoring Saint Nicholas, the patron saint of Lorraine (and children).
historicalNote: Older and more important in this region than Christmas itself. Features 'Père Fouettard' (the whipping father).

name: Fête de la Saint-Louis
description: Water jousting tournament in the canals of Sète, known as the 'Venice of Languedoc'.
historicalNote: Celebrating the city's patron saint (Louis XIV) since 1666. Jousters combat on boats to the sound of oboes.

name: Fête des Vendanges de Montmartre
description: Celebrating the wine harvest from the last remaining vineyard in Paris.
historicalNote: Since 1934. The 'Clos Montmartre' vineyard produces about 1,000 bottles auctioned for charity.
